abstract: With the increasing value generated through data curation and the rise of artificial intelligence (AI) agents that act as human agents, vendor companies in established business-to-business relationships increasingly delegate data curation tasks to algorithmic data requesters (ADRs) instead of human data requesters (HDRs). Using a randomized field experiment with a European pharmaceutical company and a follow-up online experiment, we show how customers respond to ADRs versus HDRs across two tasks: data enrichment (i.e., adding new information) and data reconciliation (i.e., updating existing records). For data enrichment, customers are more likely to agree and complete requests sent by ADRs because they expect lower effort. For data reconciliation requests, customers lean toward HDRs, reflecting stronger accuracy concerns. Interestingly, we observe only a marginal advantage in completion rates for HDRs. These findings advise vendors to match agents to data curation tasks; they should deploy ADRs for data enrichment to reduce customer burden, and they should use HDRs for data reconciliation to address error concerns. Relatedly, vendors should craft emails that match the messaging context; they should frame data enrichment messages around convenience and benefits, and they should frame data reconciliation messages around accuracy, auditability, and risk reduction.

ob.ai_reason: 算法数据请求者 vs 人类，AI核心
ob.tldr: 研究B2B关系中客户对人类数据请求者(HDR)与算法数据请求者(ADR)的响应差异。基于欧洲药企的田野实验和在线实验发现：数据富化任务中，客户更愿配合ADR，因感知努力更低；数据调解任务中，客户偏好HDR，反映准确性顾虑。完成率差异实际较小。管理启示：企业应根据任务类型匹配代理类型——数据富化部署ADR降低客户负担，数据调解使用HDR应对错误担忧；并相应调整邮件框架：富化强调便利收益，调解强调准确性与风险管控。

title: Agent-Based Data Curation Practices: Customer Responses to Human versus Algorithmic Data Requesters in Established Business-to-Business Relationships
title_zh: 基于代理的数据整理实践：既定企业对企业关系中客户对人工与算法数据请求者的响应
